question:
Will Elon Musk post 340-359 tweets from November 4 to November 11, 2025?

Description: This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from November 4 12:00 PM ET to November 11, 2025 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://www.xtracker.io/. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

Resolution source: https://x.com/elonmusk

evidences.title: Polymarket XTracker API and Elon Musk Tweet Count Markets
evidences.content: Counting rules and resolution are consistent across markets that use the tracker: main feed posts, quote posts, and reposts count; standard replies do not unless they appear in the main feed view; deleted posts count if captured before removal, roughly within five minutes. The Post Counter on xtracker.polymarket.com is the primary source, with X itself as a backup if the tracker has a gap. Because the post list is exportable, users can audit the count.
evidences.query: xtracker.io Elon Musk Post Counter export data main feed quote repost replies counted rules deleted posts tracker secondary resolution X

evidences.title: Polymarket Elon Tracker
evidences.content: The bot uses xTracker.io as its primary data source and follows the same counting rules as official Polymarket resolution: main feed posts, quote posts, and reposts count, and captured deleted posts can count, with X itself as a backup source for verification.

evidences.title: Profiling Elon Musk's Twitter/X evolution: Political Thought, Public ...
evidences.content: ## Description This report examines the evolution of Elon Musk’s Twitter (now X) presence from 2010 to early 2025, analyzing his posting behavior, ideological shifts, emotional tone, and influence on public discourse. We used advanced computational text analysis techniques, including emotion detection, personality profiling (Big Five model), and political ideology classification. We traced how Musk's communication style transformed from predominantly neutral and technology-centric discourse into a more politically engaged and emotionally charged presence. The study highlights key trends, such as increased ideologically polarized content, heightened engagement patterns for controversial posts, and shifts in personality traits correlated with significant real-world events. [...] Skip to main Published March 21, 2025 | Version v2 Report Open # Profiling Elon Musk's Twitter/X evolution: Political Thought, Public Persona and Influence ### Authors/Creators Jones, Marc (Researcher)1 ORCID icon Mikros, George (Researcher)2 ORCID icon 1. ROR icon Northwestern University 2. Hamad Bin Khalifa University ## Description [...] The report comes with two datasets in CSV files: Dataset 1: The Co...

evidences.title: Elon Musk definitely tweets too much
evidences.content: Furthermore, Twitter lists timestamps in the GMT timezone. For all figures except those that explicitly require timestamps and are sensitive to exact hour/time, I convert these timestamps to CST. That corresponds to the timezone in Texas, where Elon resides and spends the majority of his time. Ultimately, I was able to gather data from 7/27/24 to 9/12/24 (48 days). #### 2 thoughts on “Elon Musk definitely tweets too much” 1. Image 10 ArcosJanuary 7, 2025 at 6:13 pm Reply Nice work! Would be interesting to see the 2024 version of this including his alt accounts (Adrian Dittmann etc.) 2. Image 11 Sirius observerJanuary 13, 2025 at 9:30 pm Reply You have nailed it… [...] ( Here are some key takeaways from this article: Elon Musk tweets a lot. From 7/27/24 to 9/12/24, he hasaveraged nearly 68 tweets per day. I estimate that hespends ~2.7 hours/day on Twitterusing conservative assumptions. He constantly interacts with notoriously racist alt-right accounts, like Ian Miles Cheong (@stillgray) and EndWokeness (@endwokeness) For better or worse, Elon Musk is one of the richest and most powerful men on the planet. Through SpaceX and StarLink, he has incredible influence on American policy...

evidences.title: Tech billionaire Elon Musk's social media posts have had a 'sudden ...
evidences.content: The view counts for his posts increased by 138%, retweets by 238%, and likes by 186%. In contrast, other prominent political accounts on X saw more moderate increases: 57% in view counts, 152% in retweets, and 130% in likes. This suggests that while engagement went up for all accounts after July, Musk’s metrics saw a particularly large boost, particularly in retweets and likes. The research further found that since July, other conservative and right-wing X accounts have performed better in terms of visibility of posts compared to progressive and left-wing accounts. The Conversation sought comment from X about the research, but did not receive a reply before deadline. [...] Musk’s efforts to influence who wins next week’s US presidential election have continued. For example, over the past three months, he has donated more than US$100 million to a political action committee called America PAC that’s promoting Trump. But our new research (currently available in preprint form) indicates Musk may be wielding influence in other more subtle ways as well. However, the platform’s increasing opacity to researchers makes this difficult to say for certain. Shortly after Musk endorsed Trump’...

evidences.title: A Content Analysis of Donald Trump's and Elon Musk's Tweets
evidences.content: ## Results ### General Tweet Characteristics and Engagement Patterns #### Tweet Volume and Frequency Analysis of the 2,391 tweets reveals distinct posting behaviors between Donald Trump and Elon Musk. Trump demonstrated a higher frequency of Twitter/X/X communication, averaging 7.3 tweets per day during active periods, compared to Musk's 3.8 tweets per day (table 2). Trump's tweeting pattern showed pronounced spikes during political events and controversies, while Musk's posting frequency remained more consistent with occasional surges around product launches and company announcements. Table 2. Tweet Frequency and Volume Characteristics. evidences.title: How Traders Are Making Thousands Counting Elon Musk's ...
evidences.content: There's a market on Poly Market right now with $23 million in volume, $15 million traded in the last 24 hours. And it's not about war. It's not about an election. It's not even about crypto. It's about how many times Elon Musk is tweeting this week. 30 different brackets, 0 to 19 tweets, 20 to 39 all the way up to 580 plus. Thousands of people spending real money trying to predict the posting habits of one man. And somehow this might be the smartest market on the entire platform. Let me explain. So here's how it works. Every week, Poly Market opens a new market. How many times will Elon Musk post on X from Monday to Monday? Posts, quote tweets, and reposts all count. Replies do not, unless they show up on his main feed. The market splits into 30 brackets, each covering a 20 tweet range. [...] splits into 30 brackets, each covering a 20 tweet range. You buy the bracket you think he lands in, and if you're right, your share pays out a dollar. If you're wrong, they go to zero. Right now, the current week, February 24th to March 3rd, the market is pricing 300 to 319 tweets as the most likely outcome at 24%. The bracket below it, 280 to 299 is 22% and 320 to 339 is also 22%. So the m...

evidences.title: Musk Tweet Tracker — Polymarket Automation - GitHub
evidences.content: # Musk Tweet Tracker — Polymarket Automation Automated toolkit for trading Elon Musk tweet count markets on Polymarket. Fetches live data from xtracker.io (official resolution source) and Polymarket API, analyzes tweet patterns, forecasts totals, and builds optimal position structures. ## How It Works Polymarket hosts prediction markets on how many times Musk will tweet over a given period (2-day and 7-day windows). The market is split into ranges (e.g. "201-225 tweets"). You build a structure of positions across ranges — if the actual count lands within your coverage, you profit. This toolkit automates the analysis: ## Project Structure [...] This toolkit automates the analysis: ## Project Structure `config.py — Configuration and constants polymarket_client.py — Polymarket API client (Gamma + CLOB) tweet_tracker.py — xtracker.io scraper with local history analyzer.py — Statistical analysis: rolling avg, wave detection, forecast position_builder.py — Position structure optimizer trade_logger.py — CSV trade journal with analytics main.py — CLI dashboard tying everything together` ## Quick Start ### Modes ## Strategy Rules (encoded in config) ## API Reference | API | Base URL | Au...

evidences.title: Free Speech, Social Media, And Business Impact: A Case ...
evidences.content: Elon Musk’s social media posts have not only had an impact on public perception and the stock market, but they have also led to legal and regulatory consequences for his business. Musk’s tweets have prompted investigations by regulatory authorities such as the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), who have accused him of violating securities laws. These legal battles have resulted in hefty fines and restrictions on Musk’s activities as a CEO. Furthermore, Musk’s tweets have raised questions about the responsibility and accountability of business leaders in the digital age, leading to calls for clearer regulations regarding social media use by executives. The case study of Elon Musk’s X serves as a cautionary tale for businesses, highlighting the potential legal pitfalls and [...] Free Speech in business raises important ethical considerations. When it comes to Elon Musk’s X, these dimensions are particularly significant. Balancing the rights of free speech with the responsibilities of a business can be challenging, especially in the age of social media. Social media platforms amplify the impact of free speech, both positively and negatively, on businesses. In the case of Elo...

evidences.title: The Evolution of Twitter
evidences.content: Facebook • • • • • • Snapchat • • • • • • Twitter • • • • • LinkedIn • • • • • Skype • • • • • TikTok • • • • • • WhatsApp • • • • YouTube • • • • Pinterest • • • • • Reddit • • • Twitch • • Source: Fischer (2020, para. 1). A report by Axios based on a comparative study (Table 1) confirmed this homogenization of the different social media: “Tech platforms used to focus on ways to create wildly different products to attract audiences. Today, they all have similar features, and instead differentiate themselves with their philosophies, values and use cases” (Fischer, 2020, para. 3). The issue of platform homogenization will be revisited in the next section, where it will be connected to Elon Musk’s strategic actions as the head of X. Conclusions and Discussion Since the beginning, it was [...] a discussion on the promises and limitations of applying an evolutionary approach to media change. Keywords: Twitter, X, media evolution, media history, intermediality The recent significant shifts in the management and overall strategy of the corporation Twitter (now X), which directly impact the users’ experiences and the platform’s interactions within the media and cultural landscape, posi...

evidences.title: Discussions of Asperger Syndrome on Social Media: Content and Sentiment Analysis on Twitter
evidences.content: When we compared the engagement and sentiment of tweets before and after Musk's announcement, we found that tweets posted before the disclosure received significantly more engagement; they received more comments (254.15, 95% CI 87.1 to 331.5 compared to 44.88, 95% CI –74.6 to 493.2; _P_<.001), more retweets (494.47, 95% CI 28.3 to 635.6 compared to 190.80, 95% CI –321.6 to 928.9; _P_=.001), and more likes (7058.00, 95% CI 1734.9 to 10,443.6 compared to 969.44, 95% CI –4483.2 to 16,661.8; _P_<.001) than after the announcement (See Table 1). We did not find any statistically significant differences regarding the sentiment of tweets posted before and after Musk’s disclosure. Finally, the Kruskal-Wallis test did not show significant differences regarding engagement or sentiment according to [...] ### Data Analysis All statistical analyses were performed using SPSS (version 25.0; IBM Corp). Both the data set and data analysis, including scripts, were made available in the data repository . The treatment of data for this study was approved by the data protection officer at the University Hospital of North Norway (Nr.02489). ## tweets were posted the week before the Musk announcement a...

evidences.title: Elon Musk - Full Speech at the 2025 Annual Tesla Shareholder ...
evidences.content: # Elon Musk - Full Speech at the 2025 Annual Tesla Shareholder Meeting (6.11.2025) ## Bučko Buček 1770 subscribers 1396 likes ### Description 47120 views Posted: 9 Nov 2025 Elon Musk delivered a visionary address on November 6, 2025, at Tesla's Annual Shareholder Meeting in Austin, Texas, framing the company's future as a "new book" centered on AI, autonomy, and robotics. The speech blended product updates, ambitious targets, philosophical insights, and a strong endorsement of his $1 trillion performance-based compensation package (approved by ~75% of shareholders). Below are the major takeaways, organized by theme: 1. Optimus Humanoid Robot: The Path to Abundance [...] 2. Full Self-Driving (FSD) and Autonomy Milestones FSD is at an "inflection point," with unsupervised autonomy "effectively solved" and surpassing human reliability within months; current fleet stats show 1 crash per 6.8 million miles (10x safer than U.S. average). Upcoming: Version 14.2/14.3 enables safe "texting while driving" or sleeping en route; AI5 chip targets 1,000x human safety. Global rollout: China approval targeted for Feb/March 2026; Europe in Q1 2026. Musk urged European owners to lobby regulators....

evidences.content: hucken 6 #### good world, bad other stuff 6.5/10 to be more honest. I like the main story and the world building solid 9/10. It gives enough mysteries, but as many said, it feels like Lost. Opening up many many secrets and stuff to think about without any resolutions. Will there be any? Will they be satisfying? Will it be canceled before that? Will it be rushed? Who knows. But something feels off, acting? Writing? Directing? All of it? I can't put my finger on it but sometimes it feels like i'm watching a soap opera. Also some really annoying characters, stubborn or stereotypical characters and behaviours - it's really irritating and throws me out of the immersion, catching myself looking at my phone browsing stuff until the scene is done. 5/10.
